summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that Occupy Summer School (OSS), an educational experiment connected to the Occupy movement, has been featured in The New Yorker's Talk of the Town column. Journalist Alex Carp attended more than half of the summer school sessions and wrote up the experience. The post also mentions coverage from German Public Radio and includes a personal note identifying O'Neil as the blue-haired person in an accompanying photo. This is essentially a brief celebratory announcement sharing media coverage of the grassroots educational initiative.
key_points: Occupy Summer School received mainstream media recognition in The New Yorker
A journalist embedded with the program for an extended period to write the piece
The initiative also attracted international attention from German Public Radio
O'Neil was personally involved as a participant/organizer in the summer school
money_quotes: I'm super proud to say that Alex Carp, a journalist who was present for more than half of Occupy Summer School last month, did a fantastic job of writing up the OSS experiment
I'm the blue-haired person on the left
key_insight: Grassroots educational experiments emerging from social movements can gain legitimacy through mainstream media coverage.
